hypothesis: In MADE extended with both MLIP and DFT oracles, an agent that uses a calibrated multi-fidelity Gaussian process to model cross-fidelity correlations and selects queries by maximizing the expected number of new convex-hull phases will produce a higher yield of novel DFT-stable compounds per DFT evaluation than a single-fidelity MLIP-only BO baseline with diversity filtering, with the advantage increasing from ternary to quaternary chemical systems.
